home.features.title: Core Features
home.features.feature1Desc: Supports one-on-one, groups, and rooms with message persistence and offline sync.
home.features.feature2Desc: Compact protocol stack using binary Protobuf for efficient and secure communication.
home.features.feature3Desc: Built-in Agent endpoint with streaming responses and real-time interaction.
home.downloads.title: Downloads
home.downloads.note: For all installers, visit GitHub Release
home.whyChoose.title: Why ChatLite
home.whyChoose.lead: Not just another chat app. ChatLite is redesigned from the ground up for those who truly care about communication quality
home.whyChoose.point1Sub: Based on Protobuf + WebSocket, message payload is 45% smaller than JSON, smooth even on weak networks.
home.whyChoose.point2Sub: Messages never lost. Auto-pull history on device switch, so important conversations are always there.
home.whyChoose.point3Sub: Group chat summaries, auto-replies, message search — more than chat, a productivity tool.
home.whyChoose.point4Sub: Server-side persistent storage with offline pull. Auto-syncs message history across devices.
home.whyChoose.point5Sub: Native clients for Windows, macOS, Linux, plus web — seamless switching between devices.
home.whyChoose.point6Sub: Fully open source with one-click Docker deployment. Private messaging for your team.

faq.title: FAQ - ChatLite
faq.lead: Frequently asked questions about ChatLite, covering features, privacy, open source, and technical details.
faq.q1: How is ChatLite different from WeChat / Telegram?
faq.a1: ChatLite focuses on being lightweight, cross-platform, and open source. Unlike WeChat, ChatLite has no ads or tracking, and can be self-hosted. Compared to Telegram, ChatLite offers a cleaner web experience and built-in AI agent integration.
faq.a2: ChatLite uses binary Protobuf protocol with TLS/QUIC encryption for transmission. Messages are persistently stored on the server with 7-day offline message retrieval. For higher security, self-hosting is recommended.
faq.a3: Yes. ChatLite is fully open source with one-click Docker deployment. You can run the complete service on your own server, including MySQL, Redis, MongoDB, Kafka, and Qdrant.
faq.a4: ChatLite supports Windows (MSI), macOS (DMG), Linux (DEB), Android (APK), and all modern browsers via the web app. Messages sync in real-time across all platforms.
faq.q5: Will my chat history be lost?
faq.a5: No. Messages are persistently stored on the server with offline pull support. History auto-syncs when switching devices.
faq.q6: How does the AI assistant work?
faq.a6: ChatLite has a built-in AI Agent that supports group chat summaries, message search, smart replies, and streaming interactions. Just type naturally — no configuration needed.
faq.a7: Yes, ChatLite is completely open source. The code is hosted on GitHub — you can view, modify, and distribute it freely.
faq.q8: How can I contact the author?
faq.a8: You can submit feedback via GitHub Issues or send an email to the author (the email is shown on the homepage donation panel).
